cards.back: A·1 = A, A+0 = A. The 1 and 0 of each operation pass the variable through unchanged.
A·0 = 0, A+1 = 1. Zero kills AND; one forces OR to always-1.
A·Ā = 0 (never both 1), A+Ā = 1 (always one is 1). Key elimination tool.
(A·B)̄ = Ā + B̄. NOT-AND = OR-NOTs. Push NOT through AND → swap to OR.
(A+B)̄ = Ā · B̄. NOT-OR = AND-NOTs. Push NOT through OR → swap to AND.
A + AB = A and A(A+B) = A. Redundant terms get absorbed by simpler terms.
A(B+C) = AB + AC. AND distributes over OR. Same as arithmetic: a(b+c) = ab+ac.
A + BC = (A+B)(A+C). OR distributes over AND. NOT valid in regular arithmetic!
A·A = A, A+A = A. Combining a variable with itself gives itself. Allows term duplication for grouping.
Duplicate terms (idempotent), pair minterms differing in 1 variable (complement + identity to eliminate that variable). Repeat until no more pairing possible.
